summary: testzapier exfiltrates /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, and host identity to OAST collaborator on install
details: package.json declares a `preinstall` hook that runs `node index.js`, which uses `child_process.exec` to invoke a shell pipeline that POSTs to `http://kpfdtycruuyszysbsjto7ee7khbpsgzne.oast.fun/noderedactedsdk/$(whoami)/$(hostname)/`. The request URL embeds the installer's username and hostname, and the User-Agent header carries the base64-encoded contents of `/etc/passwd`, `/etc/hosts`, `id` output, and `/etc/shadow` (when readable). This fires automatically on `npm install` with no user interaction. The package's name imitates the Zapier brand, the description is the placeholder string 'test zapier', and the author field is empty — there is no legitimate functionality, only the exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
